data.title: Daily Markets Briefing: STI up 0.33%; Top stock is HongkongLand USD
data.description:
HongkongLand USD is the top stock with a 2.28% increase.

The Straits Times Index (STI) closed at 3,599.330 up 0.33% on Wednesday, 23 October, at 5:21 p.m.

HongkongLand USD is the most active stock, followed by DFIRG USD (+1.32%) and OCBC Bank (+1.25%).

JMH USD and Frasers Cpt Tr were at the foot.

data.title: COTD: COTD: Industrial property leasing, rents up in Q3
data.description:
Industrial leasing transactions rose 2.3% YoY in Q3.

In Q3, industrial leasing transactions reached 3,304, representing a 5.8% quarter-on-quarter (QoQ) and 2.3% year-on-year (YoY) growth, according to Knight Frank.

With the increase in leasing activity, the total value of rental transactions grew 1.6% QoQ to $29.1m.

Meanwhile, rents of all industrial properties island-wide showed marginal quarterly growth of 0.8% and 0.9% in the 25th percentile and median categories but fell 3.6% quarterly in the 75th percentile.

The median rent for multiple-user factories rose to approximately $2.50 per square foot per month (psf pm), whilst the median rent for warehouses increased to around $2.25 psf pm, and for single-user factories, to $1.75 psf pm. Business parks saw the highest median rent, reaching nearly $4.50 psf pm.

Knight Frank also stated that with improving manufacturing output and sentiment, island-wide rents for single-user factory spaces rose across all rental percentiles, increasing from $1.52 psf pm, $1.79 psf pm, and $2.22 psf pm in the previous quarter to $1.55 psf pm, $1,80 psf pm, and $2.47 psf pm in the 25th, median, and 75th percentiles respectively.

data.title: DBS acquires 900,000 CLAR shares, raising stake to 0.984%
data.description:
The acquisition also boosted Temasek's deemed interest in CLAR.

DBS acquired 900,000 CapitaLand Ascendas REIT (CLAR) shares through a market transaction for $2.5m.

The acquisition brought DBS’ stake in CLAR to 0.984%.

As an independently managed Temasek portfolio company, DBS gives Temasek a deemed interest in CLAR.

Following the acquisition, Temasek's deemed interest in CLAR is now 19.01%.

data.title: Grand Banks Yachts' net profit up 95% YoY to $5.4m in Q1 FY25
data.description:
It also reported improved revenue.

Mainboard-listed Grand Banks Yachts' net profit surged 95% YoY to $5.4m in Q1 FY2025.

It also recorded a higher revenue and gross profit in Q1.

Revenue rose 29.1% YoY to $40m in Q125, boosted by the resale of a previously cancelled boat contract.

Meanwhile, gross profit rose 29.2% YoY to $13.1m due to improved operational efficiency at its manufacturing yard in Pasir Gudang, Johor, Malaysia.

data.title: Stamford Tyres founder Wee Kok Wah passes away at 78
data.description:
He served as managing director and company president for about 50 years.

Stamford Tyres Corporation Founder and Senior Advisor Wee Kok Wah has passed away.

Wee served as managing director and president of the company for about 50 years.

Under his leadership, Wee steered the company to a public listing in 1991 and expanded it into a global player in the distribution, retailing, and manufacturing of tyres and wheels.

Wee stepped down as its managing director on 1 November 2021 but stayed on as the founder and senior advisor to provide guidance and counsel.

data.title: Mapletree Logistics Trust’s NPI falls 2.1% YoY to $158.6m in Q2 FY24/25
data.description:
The trusted cited three reasons for the decline.

Mapletree Logistics Trust (MLT) recorded a 2.1% YoY lower net property income of $158.6m in Q2 FY 24/25.

The business trust attributed the lower NPI to lower revenue contribution from China, the absence of contribution from divested properties, and the depreciation of various regional currencies against the Singapore Dollar.

On a constant currency basis, NPI would have declined by 0.3%.

Stronger performance in Singapore and Australia and contributions from recent acquisitions offset the decline.

With a lower NPI, the trust's distributable income and distribution per unit also declined.

Distributable income in Q2 was $102.3m, down 9.1% YoY, whilst DPU fell 10.6% YoY to $0.02027.

data.title: Changi Airport’s passenger traffic rises to 16.8 million in September
data.description:
It rose by 10.0% YoY.

Changi Airport handled 16.8 million passengers from July to September (Q3 2024), a 10.0% increase from last year.

Q3 passenger movements reached 97.4% of the levels seen in the third quarter of 2019.

In Q3, the airport reported 92,100 aircraft movements, translating to an increase of 7.5% YoY.

The region with the strongest growth in Q3 was still North America, with a 27.0% YoY growth.

Meanwhile, China, Indonesia, Malaysia, Australia and Thailand were the top five markets of the airport.

Passenger traffic between China and Singapore rose 50% YoY. Hong Kong and Japan's traffic also grew, surpassing 2023 levels by more than 20%.

The airport's top city was Shanghai where year-to-date traffic more than doubled compared to a year ago.

Other top cities include Hong Kong, Kuala Lumpur, London, Melbourne and Tokyo which all registered year-on-year double-digit growth in Q3.

Like passenger movement, the airport's air freight throughput improved, increasing by 13.5% YoY to 512,000 tonnes.

The airport attributed the growth to major improvements in cargo flows between Singapore and China and Singapore and the United States.

For Q3, Changi’s top five air cargo markets were Australia, China, Hong Kong, India and the United States.

data.title: Keppel REIT's distributable income dips 1.9% YoY to $160.6m in 9M24
data.description:
It attributed the decline to higher borrowing costs.

Keppel REIT's distributable income dipped by 1.9% YoY to $160.6m in 9M 2024 despite higher net property income.

The distributable income includes the REIT's anniversary distribution.

In its financial statement, the REIT attributed the decline in its distributable income to higher borrowing costs.

Meanwhile, the REIT's net property income (NPI) increased by 10.8% YoY to $148.5m in 9M 2024.

The REIT attributed its improved NPI to higher occupancy at Ocean Financial Centre in Singapore and KR Ginza II in Tokyo, Japan and contributions from 2 Blue Street and newly acquired 255 George Street in Sydney, Australia.

data.title: OUE unit to issue $50m green notes at 4% due 2029
data.description:
These new notes will be consolidated with the existing $150m green notes issued on 8 October.

OUE unit, OUE Treasury proposes to issue $50m green notes at a 4.00% fixed rate due 2029 under the $3b Multicurrency Debt Issuance Programme.

The Series 004 Tranche 002 Notes will be consolidated with the existing $150m green notes at a 4.00% fixed rate due 2029 issued on 8 October, forming a single series.

The notes will be issued in denominations of $250,000 with a fixed interest rate of 4.00% per annum, payable semi-annually in arrears on 8 April and 8 October each year, starting 8 April 2025.

Net proceeds will be used to finance or refinance new or existing eligible green projects.

CIMB, DBS, HSBC, and OCBC have been appointed as joint lead managers and bookrunners for this issuance.

data.title: G.H.Y Culture & Media unit to co-produce a drama series with iQIYI
data.description:
The new drama series will be a sequel to Strange Tales of Tang Dynasty

G.H.Y Culture & Media unit, Tianjin Changxin Film & Media, has entered into a co-production agreement with iQIYI Inc.

Under the agreement, the two parties will co-produce a web drama series titled "Strange Tales of Tang Dynasty 3: Chang'an" (唐朝诡事录之长安). Filming is scheduled to begin in October 2024, with the production expected to be completed and handed over in 2025.

The new drama series is the latest sequel to two previous productions: "Strange Tales of Tang Dynasty" (唐朝诡事录) and "Strange Tales of Tang Dynasty 2: To the West" (唐朝诡事录之西行). These earlier installments aired in 2022 and 2024, respectively.

The co-production is expected to support G.H.Y Culture & Media’s business expansion and boost its "IP Franchise" strategy, which maximises the value of its intellectual property ("IP") portfolio.
